title: TRUE ROMANCE
logline: A comic book store clerk falls for a call girl, kills her pimp to free her, accidentally steals a suitcase of cocaine, and flees to Los Angeles to sell it to a movie producer—pursued by the mob and caught between cops in an explosive hotel shootout.
antagonistOrConflict: Drexl Spivey (pimp), Vincent Coccotti and the Detroit mob, and the structural tension between Clarence's romantic idealism and the violent criminal world he's entered
tone: Pulpy, violent, romantic, pop-culture saturated, darkly comic with sudden bursts of extreme brutality
themes: Pop culture as identity and mythology

actBreaks.description: Clarence returns home with Alabama after killing Drexl and accidentally taking the cocaine, fundamentally transforming from lonely romantic to fugitive criminal.

validation.ruling: Scene #25 (p42, 31.8%) ends Act 1 with Clarence returning home and revealing 'I killed him' to Alabama, then eating ravenously while she cries. This irreversible act—the murder of Drexl—fundamentally transforms the story from romance to crime thriller and forces both characters into a new, higher-stakes reality they cannot escape.

actBreaks.description: Dick agrees to help broker the drug deal and the plan is set in motion, shifting from setup and flight to active execution of the sale.

validation.ruling: Scene #81 (p101, 76.5%) shows Dick receiving the call that he got the T.J. Hooker role just as they're leaving for the drug deal. This moment represents the final beat before the climactic sequence begins, closing Act 2 with Dick's character arc completing and the team heading into the final confrontation at the Beverly Wilshire.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality: This script operates on a two-hinge structure that's deceptively simple but emotionally complex. Act One is a compressed origin story—Clarence meets Alabama, discovers she's a call girl hired for his birthday, marries her anyway, then kills her pimp in a scene that's both horrifying and weirdly heroic. The cocaine is almost an afterthought, a MacGuffin grabbed by accident. Act Two sprawls across the geography and introduces multiple threat vectors: the mob tracking them through Clarence's father, the cops using Dick's friend Elliot as an informant, and Alabama's brutal beating that tests their bond. The structure is road-movie meets crime-thriller, with Tarantino using the journey from Detroit to LA as both literal escape and metaphorical transformation. Act Three compresses time radically—the entire hotel shootout sequence occupies nearly 30 pages and 40+ scenes, using rapid cross-cutting between multiple locations to build unbearable tension before the explosive climax.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The pacing is wildly uneven by design. The first 42 pages establish character through extended dialogue scenes—the bar conversation, the all-night date, the rooftop proposal. Then scene 24 (Drexl's confrontation) erupts in sudden violence. The middle act alternates between leisurely character moments (the zoo meeting, scenes 45-46) and mounting dread (Coccotti interrogating Cliff, scene 38). The final act is a masterclass in cross-cutting: scenes 88-124 ping-pong between the elevator, the cops' room, Lee's room, and various hallways, with scene lengths dropping to half a page or less. This creates a staccato rhythm that mirrors the chaos of the shootout. The epilogue (scenes 127-128) then releases all tension with a sun-drenched Mexican beach fantasy.

incitingIncident.description: Alabama trips over a wine bottle and spills popcorn all over Clarence, then sits next to him and they begin talking during the movie.

validation.ruling: Scene #7 (p10, 7.6%) is the meet-cute where Alabama 'accidentally' spills popcorn on Clarence at the movie theater, shattering his solitary existence and initiating the central romance that drives all subsequent action. This irreversible encounter establishes the main relationship and sets the entire plot in motion.

midpoint.description: Alabama enters the hotel room to find Virgil sitting with a sawed-off shotgun aimed at her face, and he calmly orders her inside and demands to know where the coke and Clarence are.
midpoint.sceneHeading: INT. HOLLYWOOD HOLIDAY INN - CLARENCE'S ROOM - DAY

validation.ruling: Scene #62 occurs after the midpoint window and is a consequence of earlier decisions. Scene #38 (p60, 45.5%) is the true midpoint where Coccotti's men interrogate and torture Clarence's father Cliff, who sacrifices himself rather than reveal Clarence's location. This raises stakes dramatically, makes the conflict deeply personal, and represents the point of no return—Clarence can no longer simply walk away from the consequences of killing Drexl.

climax.description: The Mexican standoff continues with gangsters, bodyguards, and cops all aiming weapons at each other; Elliot tries to leave but is told to stay, and Lee realizes Elliot knew Dimes' name and threatens him.
climax.sceneHeading: INT. BEVERLY WILSHIRE - LEE'S HOTEL ROOM - DAY

validation.ruling: Scene #121 shows aftermath and Alabama crawling to wounded Clarence, but the actual climax is Scene #119 (p123, 93.2%) where the Mexican standoff erupts into the ultimate confrontation—gangsters, bodyguards, and cops all have weapons drawn, Lee realizes Elliot's betrayal, and the tension reaches its absolute peak before the shootout begins. This is the unavoidable confrontation that resolves the main story question.

scenes.heading: EXT. CADILLAC BAR - TWILIGHT
scenes.summary: Gotham city rises in the background at twilight.

scenes.heading: INT. BAR - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Clarence tries to pick up an older woman named Lucy at a smoky Detroit bar, talking enthusiastically about Elvis in Jailhouse Rock, but she remains charmed yet uninterested.

scenes.heading: INT. DINGY HOTEL ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: In a dingy hotel room filled with cocaine, guns, and a TV playing Bewitched, Drexl Spivey and Floyd Dixon argue about food while Big Don arrives with hamburgers.

scenes.heading: INT. LYRIC THEATER - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Clarence watches a Sonny Chiba Streetfighter movie at the Lyric Theater, smiling with his legs over the seat in front of him.

scenes.heading: EXT. LYRIC THEATER - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Alabama arrives by cab at the Lyric Theater and buys a ticket to the triple feature already in progress.

scenes.heading: INT. LYRIC THEATER - LOBBY - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Alabama enters the theater lobby and orders popcorn, a large Mr. Pibb, and Goobers from the concession stand.

scenes.heading: INT. LYRIC THEATER
scenes.summary: Alabama trips over a wine bottle and spills popcorn all over Clarence, then sits next to him and they begin talking during the movie.
scenes.turningPointNote: Clarence and Alabama meet for the first time — the inciting incident that launches the entire romance and story.

scenes.heading: EXT. LYRIC THEATRE
scenes.summary: Outside the theater, Clarence and Alabama perform martial arts moves together and laugh, then Clarence offers to walk her to her car but learns she took a cab.

scenes.heading: INT. DENNY'S RESTAURANT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: At Denny's around 12:30 AM, Clarence asks Alabama about herself and she reveals she's from Tallahassee, her favorite movie star is Cary Grant, and she came to Detroit to be an actress.

scenes.heading: INT. COMIC BOOK STORE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Clarence takes Alabama to the comic book store where he works at 1:30 AM, showing her around and offering to let her see a Spiderman number one comic.

scenes.heading: INT. CLARENCE'S APARTMENT - BEDROOM - NIGHT (LATER)
scenes.summary: Clarence and Alabama make love while Clarence talks continuously, mentioning he wanted to say something to her at the movies but thought she wouldn't want to be bothered.

scenes.heading: INT. CLARENCE'S APARTMENT - BEDROOM - NIGHT (LATER)
scenes.summary: In a post-sex bath, Clarence and Alabama discuss Janis Joplin's death, with Clarence explaining she died from bad heroin, not an intentional overdose.

scenes.heading: INT. CLARENCE'S LIVING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: The next day, Clarence wakes up alone and sees the skylight to the roof is open, then puts on a fur coat and heads up.

scenes.heading: EXT. CLARENCE'S APARTMENT (DOWNTOWN DETROIT) - ROOF
scenes.summary: On the roof at dawn, Alabama tearfully confesses to Clarence that she's a call girl who was paid to be at the theater as a birthday present for him, but she fell in love with him.
scenes.turningPointNote: Alabama reveals the truth about being hired as a call girl — a devastating confession that could destroy their relationship but instead deepens it when Clarence accepts her.

scenes.heading: EXT. TATTOO PARLOR - DAY
scenes.summary: Clarence's car sits with two wheels up on the curb outside a seedy tattoo parlor.

scenes.heading: INT. TATTOO PARLOR - CLOSE ON NEEDLE - DAY
scenes.summary: Alabama gets a tattoo of Clarence's name on her butt while telling him about her sexual history, including losing her virginity at sixteen and becoming a call girl after her boyfriend died.

scenes.heading: INT. CLARENCE'S APARTMENT - BATHROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: In the bathroom, Clarence splashes water on his face and Elvis Presley appears, telling him to shoot Drexl in the face and feed him to the dogs to get unhaunted.
scenes.turningPointNote: Clarence decides to kill Drexl after a hallucinatory conversation with Elvis — the decision that propels him into the violent criminal world.

scenes.heading: INT. CLARENCE'S APARTMENT - BEDROOM - CLOSE ON SNUBNOSEED
scenes.summary: Clarence loads a snub-nosed .38 revolver and sticks it in a heavy athletic sock.

scenes.heading: INT. CLARENCE'S APARTMENT - LIVING ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Clarence asks Alabama to write down Drexl's address so he can pick up her things, and despite her fear, he insists Drexl poses no threat to them.

scenes.heading: INT/EXT. RED MUSTANG - DOWNTOWN DETROIT STREETS - TWILIGHT
scenes.summary: Clarence drives his red Mustang through decayed Victorian mansions in a dangerous Detroit neighborhood at twilight, heading to the address Alabama gave him.

scenes.heading: EXT. DOWNTOWN DETROIT STREET - TWILIGHT
scenes.summary: Clarence steps out of his car in a dangerous area at night, checks his racing pulse, and does an Elvis gyration to pump himself up before walking into an open field.

scenes.heading: EXT. NO-MAN'S LAND FIELD - TWILIGHT
scenes.summary: Clarence walks through a field where crack pipes glow in the background, and a dealer named Jupiter approaches offering drugs before Clarence asks for Drexl's house.

scenes.heading: EXT. DARK VICTORIAN - TWILIGHT
scenes.summary: Clarence knocks on Drexl's door, tells Marty he needs to talk to Drexl about Alabama, and Drexl appears asking where that bitch is.

scenes.heading: INT. DREXL'S LIVING ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Drexl and Marty walk into the living room continuing their conversation about rock whores while Clarence follows, then Drexl sits down to eat Chinese food and casually asks Clarence if Alabama sent him to get her clothes.
scenes.turningPointNote: The confrontation with Drexl escalates to violence — Clarence shoots Drexl multiple times, killing him, and accidentally takes a suitcase of cocaine instead of Alabama's belongings.

scenes.heading: INT. CLARENCE'S APARTMENT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Clarence returns home and tells Alabama he killed Drexl, then brings in take-out food and eats ravenously while explaining the shooting was self-defense after Drexl pulled a gun.

scenes.heading: EXT. CLIFF'S CAR - MOVING - MORNING
scenes.summary: A big white Chevy Nova drives down the road at sunrise.

scenes.heading: INT. CLIFF'S CAR - MOVING - MORNING
scenes.summary: Clifford Worley drives home from his security guard job singing gently to the sunrise and sipping coffee.

scenes.heading: EXT. TRAILER PARK - MORNING
scenes.summary: Cliff pulls into his trailer park and sees Clarence and Alabama waiting for him, causing a tear to roll down his cheek as Clarence greets him.

scenes.heading: INT. TRAILER HOME - MORNING
scenes.summary: Inside the trailer, Clarence introduces Alabama as his wife, asks his father for a beer, and begins telling him about recent events while Cliff tries to process the surprise visit.

scenes.heading: INT. OUTSIDE OF CASTING DIRECTOR'S OFFICE - DAY
scenes.summary: Dick Ritchie waits with other actors outside a casting director's office and is called in to audition.

scenes.heading: INT. CASTING DIRECTOR'S OFFICE - DAY
scenes.summary: Dick auditions for the part of Marty in a T.J. Hooker episode, reading a scene where he's trying to shake William Shatner off a car hood, and the casting director smiles at his performance.

scenes.heading: INT. TRAILER HOME - DAY
scenes.summary: Cliff is aghast after Clarence tells him something heavy, threatens to go out of his head if Clarence makes a joke, then asks what Clarence needs and whether he still has friends on the police force.

scenes.heading: EXT. TRAILER HOME - DAY
scenes.summary: Outside the trailer, Cliff tells Clarence the police think Drexl's death is drug-related and have nothing on him, mentioning Drexl's association with gangster Blue Lou Boyle, then advises Clarence to leave town.
scenes.turningPointNote: Cliff confirms the police have no leads and advises Clarence to flee to California — setting the story's geographic shift and escalating stakes.

scenes.heading: INT. DICK'S APARTMENT - DAY
scenes.summary: Dick answers his ringing phone in his apartment.

scenes.heading: INT. HOTEL SUITE (LAS VEGAS) - SUNSET
scenes.summary: Clarence calls Dick from a Las Vegas hotel room with Alabama behind him, tells Dick he's coming to L.A. tomorrow with his wife, and hangs up before Dick can process the news.

scenes.heading: INT. DICK'S APARTMENT - DAY
scenes.summary: Dick hears the click on the other end, hangs up confused, then goes through his mail and finds a letter from Clarence.

scenes.heading: EXT. TRAILER PARK - DAY
scenes.summary: Cliff's white Chevy Nova pulls into the Astro World trailer park and he gets out drinking from a fast food cup, then opens his trailer door.

scenes.heading: INT. TRAILER - DAY
scenes.summary: A gun is pressed to Cliff's temple as he enters, he's shoved into his living room where Vincenzo Coccotti and four other men are waiting, and Coccotti interrogates him about his son while threatening torture and death.
scenes.turningPointNote: Coccotti and the mob find Cliff and torture him for information about Clarence — the moment the Detroit mob enters the story as the primary antagonist, raising stakes to life-or-death.

scenes.heading: EXT. HIGHWAY - DAY
scenes.summary: The red Mustang enters Los Angeles.

scenes.heading: INT. DICK'S APARTMENT - MORNING
scenes.summary: Dick wakes up in his recliner as Clarence and Alabama knock on his door singing, then Alabama hugs Dick enthusiastically.

scenes.heading: INT. PINK'S HOT DOG STAND - DAY
scenes.summary: Pink's Hot Dog Stand employees assemble hot dogs like skilled chefs.

scenes.heading: EXT. PINK'S HOT DOG STAND - PATIO - DAY
scenes.summary: At Pink's outdoor patio, Alabama tells Clarence and Dick the story of how she was born in a bus after her father crashed the car, and was named after the state where the bus driver was from.

scenes.heading: EXT. HOLLYWOOD HOLIDAY INN - DAY
scenes.summary: Clarence's empty red Mustang is parked in the Hollywood Holiday Inn parking lot.

scenes.heading: INT. HOLLYWOOD HOLIDAY INN - CLARENCE'S ROOM - CLOSEUP -
scenes.summary: Clarence shows Dick a suitcase full of cocaine in his hotel room, Dick tests it and confirms it's real, then Clarence explains he wants to sell it and asks Dick to find a buyer.
scenes.turningPointNote: Clarence reveals the cocaine to Dick and enlists him to find a buyer — launching the drug deal plot that will drive the rest of the film.

scenes.heading: EXT. LOS ANGELES ZOO - CLOSEUP - PANTHER - DAY
scenes.summary: At the L.A. Zoo, Dick introduces Clarence and Alabama to actor Elliot Blitzer, and Clarence aggressively pitches the cocaine deal while Elliot asks where it came from.

scenes.heading: EXT. LOS ANGELES ZOO - SNACK BAR - DAY
scenes.summary: At the zoo snack bar, Clarence tells Elliot he doesn't look like a beautiful blonde with big tits, then explains he needs the money to start a new life and insists Elliot call his connection now.

scenes.heading: INT. PORSCHE (MULHOLLAND DRIVE) - MOVING - DAY
scenes.summary: Movie producer Lee Donowitz drives his Porsche through the Hollywood Hills when his car phone rings and Elliot calls him on a Sunday.

scenes.heading: EXT. LOS ANGELES ZOO - SNACK BAR - DAY
scenes.summary: At the zoo pay phone, Elliot tells Lee he's with the party Lee wanted him to meet, while Clarence, Dick, and Alabama stand next to him.

scenes.heading: INT. PORSCHE - MOVING - DAY
scenes.summary: Lee asks Elliot why he's calling his phone to talk about that.

scenes.heading: EXT. LOS ANGELES ZOO - SNACK BAR - DAY
scenes.summary: Elliot tells Lee that Clarence is there right now and insists on talking to him.

scenes.heading: INT. PORSCHE (IN TUNNEL) - MOVING - DAY
scenes.summary: Lee's voice echoes in the tunnel as he asks if Clarence is out of his mind.

scenes.heading: EXT. LOS ANGELES ZOO - SNACK BAR - DAY
scenes.summary: Elliot tries to relay a message but Clarence takes the phone and greets Lee, saying they finally meet.

scenes.heading: EXT. DICK'S APARTMENT - DAY
scenes.summary: Virgil knocks on Dick's door and Floyd answers, revealing that Clarence and Alabama aren't staying there but he knows who Virgil is talking about.

scenes.heading: EXT. LOS ANGELES ZOO - SNACK BAR - DAY
scenes.summary: Clarence tells Lee on the phone that he wants to open Dr. Zhivago in L.A. and wants Lee to distribute it.

scenes.heading: INT. PORSCHE - MOVING - DAY
scenes.summary: Lee, stopped in traffic on Sunset Boulevard, says Dr. Zhivago is a pretty big movie.

scenes.heading: EXT. LOS ANGELES ZOO - SNACK BAR - DAY
scenes.summary: Clarence tells Lee this is the biggest movie he's ever dealt with and that Lee is no idiot to be cautious.

scenes.heading: INT. PORSCHE - MOVING - DAY
scenes.summary: Lee explains he's a film producer first, not primarily a distributor, and has a select group he works with for easy business.

scenes.heading: EXT. LOS ANGELES ZOO - SNACK BAR - DAY
scenes.summary: Clarence counters that Lee's usual movies are risky while Dr. Zhivago will pack theaters for two years straight.

scenes.heading: INT. PORSCHE - MOVING - DAY
scenes.summary: Lee asks why the hurry and whether the rights to Dr. Zhivago are in arbitration.

scenes.heading: EXT. LOS ANGELES ZOO - SNACK BAR - DAY
scenes.summary: Clarence says he needs to announce the deal at Cannes and tells Lee to come to terms with his fear and desire, then hands the phone to Elliot who confirms he knows Dick from acting class.

scenes.heading: EXT. HOLIDAY INN - DAY
scenes.summary: Clarence and Alabama arrive at the Holiday Inn, she asks if she did okay, he praises her performance, and she heads inside to bathe while he goes to get dinner.

scenes.heading: INT. HOLLYWOOD HOLIDAY INN - CLARENCE'S ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Alabama enters the hotel room to find Virgil sitting with a sawed-off shotgun aimed at her face, and he calmly orders her inside and demands to know where the coke and Clarence are.
scenes.turningPointNote: The mob catches up with Alabama — the violent consequences of stealing the cocaine arrive in full force

scenes.heading: INT. RED MUSTANG - MOVING - DAY
scenes.summary: Clarence drives to get food, singing happily to himself about Hollywood.

scenes.heading: INT. HOLLYWOOD HOLIDAY INN - CLARENCE'S ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Alabama lies on the floor after blacking out from being hit, wakes to see Virgil smiling, and realizes she's going to die because there's no way she'll tell him anything.

scenes.heading: INT. HAMBURGER STAND - DAY
scenes.summary: Clarence enters a hamburger stand and orders two double chili cheeseburgers, chili fries, diet Cokes, and a combination burrito.

scenes.heading: INT. HOLLYWOOD HOLIDAY INN - CLARENCE'S ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Virgil throws Alabama into a corner, tells her Clarence wouldn't go through this for her, and delivers spinning roundhouse kicks to her head when she refuses to answer his questions.

scenes.heading: INT. HAMBURGER STAND - CLOSEUP - BURGERS - DAY
scenes.summary: Clarence waits for his burger order and strikes up a conversation with a customer about a Newsweek issue featuring Elvis.

scenes.heading: INT. HOLLYWOOD HOLIDAY INN - CLARENCE'S ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Virgil beats Alabama while monologuing about killing, finds the black suitcase of cocaine under the mattress, and Alabama crawls to her purse, pulls out a corkscrew, and stabs him repeatedly until he falls dead.
scenes.turningPointNote: Alabama kills Virgil in brutal self-defense — a transformative act of violence that saves her life and protects the cocaine deal

scenes.heading: EXT. HOLLYWOOD HOLIDAY INN - DAY
scenes.summary: Hotel guests watch from outside their rooms as Clarence walks through the pool area carrying his food bundle while sirens approach.

scenes.heading: EXT. RED MUSTANG - MOVING - DAY
scenes.summary: Clarence drives frantically with the unconscious, muttering Alabama in the passenger seat while stroking her hair and apologizing.

scenes.heading: EXT. MOTEL 6 - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Clarence's red Mustang is parked outside a new motel at night.

scenes.heading: INT. MOTEL 6 - CLARENCE'S ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Alabama sleeps in bed with a fat lip and black and blue face.

scenes.heading: INT. NOWHERE - ?
scenes.summary: Clarence speaks directly to camera about feeling horrible for what Alabama went through and how her loyalty during the beating proved her love was real, not a mistake.

scenes.heading: EXT. HOLLYWOOD HILLS - NIGHT
scenes.summary: A graceful shot of the Hollywood hills at night with the roar of a car engine heard in the distance.

scenes.heading: EXT. MULHOLLAND DRIVE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: A silver Porsche drives recklessly on Mulholland Drive, taking quick corners and pushing to the edge.

scenes.heading: INT. SILVER PORSCHE - MOVING - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Elliot drives his Porsche wildly with a coke whore passenger until cops pull them over, and she refuses to hide his cocaine sample in her purse or bra despite his frantic pleas.

scenes.heading: INT. POLICE STATION - INTERROGATION ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Two detectives, Nicholson and Dimes, interrogate Elliot about the baggie of uncut cocaine they found, telling him he's caught and needs to cooperate.

scenes.heading: INT. POLICE STATION - CAPTAIN KRINKLE'S OFFICE - DAY
scenes.summary: Captain Krinkle calls in Nicholson and Dimes, who excitedly tell him they've stumbled onto a major cocaine deal and want to set up a sting operation.
scenes.turningPointNote: The police discover the drug deal and begin setting up a sting — law enforcement enters the story as a third threat

scenes.heading: INT. RESTAURANT - DAY
scenes.summary: Coccotti meets with four wise guys in Detroit, discussing Virgil's disappearance with the narcotics, while intercut scenes show Nicholson and Dimes convincing Krinkle to let them wire Elliot for the drug deal.

scenes.heading: EXT. IMPERIAL HIGHWAY - SUNRISE
scenes.summary: Clarence and Alabama sit on an abandoned sofa watching planes take off at LAX, and Clarence explains how he used to hate airports when he was poor but now loves them because they represent freedom and possibility.

scenes.heading: INT. DICK'S APARTMENT - DAY
scenes.summary: Clarence, Alabama, and Dick prepare to leave for the drug deal when the phone rings for Dick, and the caller warns him that the cops know about the deal and are setting up a bust.
scenes.turningPointNote: Dick receives a warning that the cops know about the deal — the protagonists learn they're walking into a police trap

scenes.heading: EXT. HOTEL/LAX - DAY
scenes.summary: The camera moves in closer on a hotel near LAX airport.

scenes.heading: INT. HOTEL/LAX - ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Lenny assembles a shotgun on a bed, Toothpick Vic brings him shells, Marvin cocks his shotgun, and Frankie emerges from the bathroom twirling two .45 automatics.

scenes.heading: INT. BEVERLY WILSHIRE - COPS' HOTEL ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Nicholson and Dimes wire Elliot for the sting operation while he nervously recites Shakespeare, and they test the equipment with the Internal Affairs officers.

scenes.heading: INT. RED MUSTANG - MOVING - DAY
scenes.summary: Dick asks about Alabama's beat-up face and she claims she got elbowed playing basketball, then Clarence and Alabama kiss at a red light.

scenes.heading: INT. CADILLAC - MOVING - DAY
scenes.summary: Marvin, Frankie, Lenny, and Dario ride in a rented Cadillac.

scenes.heading: EXT. BEVERLY WILSHIRE - PARKING LOT - DAY
scenes.summary: Clarence, Alabama, and Dick arrive at the Beverly Wilshire parking lot, Clarence pulls out a .45 pistol, Dick questions why he brought it, and Clarence suggests they forget the whole deal before deciding to go through with it.

scenes.heading: INT. BEVERLY WILSHIRE - LOBBY - DAY
scenes.summary: Elliot nervously waits in the lobby, Clarence approaches with the suitcase, they shake hands, and Alabama and Dick join them.

scenes.heading: INT. BEVERLY WILSHIRE - COPS' HOTEL ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Nicholson, Dimes, and other detectives listen to the wire transmission as Clarence, Elliot, Dick, and Alabama's conversation comes through the tape machine.

scenes.heading: INT. BEVERLY WILSHIRE - ELEVATOR - DAY
scenes.summary: The four ride up in the elevator alone with muzak playing, and Clarence suddenly tells Elliot to get on his knees, then hits the stop button and pulls out the .45.

scenes.heading: INT. BEVERLY WILSHIRE - COPS' HOTEL ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: The cops listening to the wire panic, debating whether Clarence knows about the wire or is bluffing.

scenes.heading: INT. BEVERLY WILSHIRE - ELEVATOR - DAY
scenes.summary: Clarence presses the .45 against Elliot's forehead and demands he admit Clarence is the dumbest person in the world, then asks what's waiting for them upstairs.

scenes.heading: INT. BEVERLY WILSHIRE - COPS' HOTEL ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Dimes says they need to get Elliot out but Nicholson points out he's in an elevator.

scenes.heading: INT. BEVERLY WILSHIRE - ELEVATOR - DAY
scenes.summary: Clarence threatens to shoot Elliot in the face if he doesn't reveal what's waiting upstairs.

scenes.heading: INT. BEVERLY WILSHIRE - COPS' HOTEL ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Nicholson insists Clarence is bluffing while Dimes worries he's going to kill Elliot.

scenes.heading: INT. BEVERLY WILSHIRE - ELEVATOR - DAY
scenes.summary: Clarence orders Elliot to stand and keeps the gun pressed to his forehead, warning that if anything goes wrong Elliot will be shot first.

scenes.heading: INT. BEVERLY WILSHIRE - COPS' HOTEL ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Nicholson insists Clarence is bluffing and doesn't know anything, urging Elliot to act.

scenes.heading: INT. BEVERLY WILSHIRE - ELEVATOR - DAY
scenes.summary: Clarence prepares to shoot Elliot by covering the gun to shield from splatter, and Elliot breaks down crying into the wire, saying he wants to go home and doesn't like this.
scenes.turningPointNote: Elliot breaks under pressure and reveals the setup to Clarence through the wire — the sting operation is exposed and collapses

scenes.heading: INT. BEVERLY WILSHIRE - COPS' HOTEL ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Nicholson and Dimes shake their heads with defeated expressions, realizing their operation is blown.

scenes.heading: INT. BEVERLY WILSHIRE - ELEVATOR - DAY
scenes.summary: Clarence lowers the gun, hugs Elliot, apologizes for scaring him, and they shake hands as friends while Dick and Alabama feel relieved.

scenes.heading: INT. BEVERLY WILSHIRE - COPS' HOTEL ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Nicholson and Dimes listen in disbelief to what they're hearing over surveillance.

scenes.heading: INT. DICK'S APARTMENT - DAY
scenes.summary: Four wiseguys with sawed-off shotguns burst into Dick's apartment and Floyd immediately tells them Clarence is at the Beverly Wilshire and gives them directions.
scenes.turningPointNote: The mob discovers Clarence's location, setting up the inevitable collision between all parties at the hotel.

scenes.heading: INT. BEVERLY WILSHIRE - LEE'S ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Clarence and his group arrive at Lee's luxurious hotel room where two armed bodyguards greet them, and when Monty tries to pat him down Clarence reveals he's carrying a .45 automatic.

scenes.heading: INT. BEVERLY WILSHIRE - COPS' HOTEL ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Dimes comments sarcastically on Clarence's verbose style during the drug deal.

scenes.heading: INT. BEVERLY WILSHIRE - LEE'S HOTEL ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Clarence discusses Vietnam films with Lee, who appreciates his perspective, then Lee asks to see the cocaine.

scenes.heading: INT. BEVERLY WILSHIRE - COP'S HOTEL ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Nicholson expresses relief that the deal is moving forward.

scenes.heading: INT. BEVERLY WILSHIRE - LEE'S HOTEL ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Clarence puts the suitcase on the table and tells Lee he's going to be impressed.

scenes.heading: INT. BEVERLY WILSHIRE - LOBBY - DAY
scenes.summary: The four wiseguys bribe the front desk clerk with a hundred dollars while posing as Warner Brothers employees to get Donowitz's room number.

scenes.heading: INT. BEVERLY WILSHIRE - LEE'S HOTEL ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Lee examines and samples the cocaine while calmly drinking coffee with Clarence, both adding cream and sugar.

scenes.heading: INT. BEVERLY WILSHIRE - COP'S HOTEL ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Nicholson worries they'll only have a possession charge if Lee doesn't make an offer, and Dimes tells him to convince Lee.

scenes.heading: INT. BEVERLY WILSHIRE - LEE'S HOTEL ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Clarence explains he's selling the cocaine cheap because he's an amateur who doesn't want to become a drug dealer, comparing himself to someone who found the Hope Diamond and just wants to sell it quickly.

scenes.heading: INT. BEVERLY WILSHIRE - COPS' HOTEL ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Nicholson and Dimes celebrate triumphantly with a hand gesture, believing they've secured the deal.

scenes.heading: INT. BEVERLY WILSHIRE - ELEVATOR - DAY
scenes.summary: The four wiseguys ride the elevator up.

scenes.heading: INT. BEVERLY WILSHIRE - LEE'S HOTEL ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Lee asks about Alabama and Dick's roles, then has Monty bring in a briefcase of money and asks if Clarence wants to count it, but Clarence asks to use the bathroom instead.

scenes.heading: INT. BEVERLY WILSHIRE - COP'S HOTEL ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Dimes and the cops stand up and prepare to make the arrest.

scenes.heading: INT. BEVERLY WILSHIRE - LEE'S HOTEL ROOM - BATHROOM
scenes.summary: Clarence enters the bathroom, does a celebratory twist, starts urinating, then hears Elvis snapping his fingers and praising him for being cool.

scenes.heading: INT. BEVERLY WILSHIRE - LEE'S HOTEL ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Nicholson, Dimes, and four detectives burst in with guns drawn, Boris and Monty refuse to drop their Uzis, and both sides threaten each other in a standoff.
scenes.turningPointNote: The drug deal explodes into a three-way armed standoff between cops, bodyguards, and the incoming mob, with all parties refusing to back down.

scenes.heading: INT. BEVERLY WILSHIRE - LEE'S HOTEL ROOM - BATHROOM
scenes.summary: Elvis reassures Clarence that Lee genuinely likes him because his praise is genuine, then fixes Clarence's collar and tells him he likes him.

scenes.heading: INT. BEVERLY WILSHIRE - LEE'S HOTEL ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: The Mexican standoff continues with gangsters, bodyguards, and cops all aiming weapons at each other; Elliot tries to leave but is told to stay, and Lee realizes Elliot knew Dimes' name and threatens him.

scenes.heading: EXT. BEVERLY WILSHIRE - DAY
scenes.summary: Cop cars arrive in pairs at the front of the hotel.

scenes.heading: INT. BEVERLY WILSHIRE - LEE'S HOTEL ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Alabama crawls toward Clarence; Dick tosses the cocaine suitcase in the air and Marvin shoots it, filling the room with white powder; Dick runs out followed by an IA officer; Lenny escapes; Alabama reaches the wounded Clarence who has blood in his eyes.
scenes.turningPointNote: The standoff erupts into chaos with gunfire, the cocaine destroyed, characters fleeing, and Clarence critically wounded—the climactic violence that determines survival.

scenes.heading: INT. BEVERLY WILSHIRE - HALLWAY - DAY
scenes.summary: Frankie runs into uniformed police in the hallway, fires his .45s and hits two officers before being shot to death.

scenes.heading: INT. BEVERLY WILSHIRE - ANOTHER HALLWAY - DAY
scenes.summary: Dick runs down a hallway pursued by an IA officer who orders him to freeze; a shotgun blast kills the IA officer and Dick keeps running while Lenny chases after him.

scenes.heading: INT. BEVERLY WILSHIRE - LEE'S HOTEL ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Dimes and Wurlitzer order Marvin to surrender; he tosses out his shotgun but refuses to come out fearing they'll shoot him; they argue back and forth while Boris calls for an ambulance and Alabama begs them not to let Clarence die.

scenes.heading: INT. BEVERLY WILSHIRE - LOBBY - DAY
scenes.summary: Lenny holds a woman hostage with a shotgun in the chaotic lobby, demanding a car, plane, and a million dollars while fifteen cops aim guns at him; Clarence and Alabama emerge from the elevator and hobble through the lobby.

scenes.heading: EXT. BEVERLY WILSHIRE - PARKING LOT - DAY
scenes.summary: Alabama and the delirious Clarence walk past police cars and firetrucks with a suitcase full of cash, get into the red Mustang, and drive away.
scenes.turningPointNote: Clarence and Alabama escape with the money, surviving the massacre and achieving their goal against all odds.

scenes.heading: INT. RED MUSTANG (CALIFORNIA COAST) - MOVING - DAY
scenes.summary: Clarence sleeps with his bandaged face in Alabama's lap as she drives pensively, thinking the words 'You're so cool' repeatedly, and they pass through the Tijuana border gate into Mexico.

scenes.heading: EXT. MEXICO BEACH - DAY
scenes.summary: On a Mexico beach, Alabama and Clarence (now wearing an eyepatch) pick up their young son Elvis and walk along the water while Alabama reflects in voiceover that she would have survived Clarence's death and still named their son Elvis.
scenes.turningPointNote: The epilogue reveals Clarence survived, they built a life together in Mexico with a son, completing their romantic escape from the criminal world.
